MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-02-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.DEFENDANT ESTELLA SAKULTANAPANICH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM TARGET NATIONAL BANK, FOR SANCTIONS, AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR PRECLUSION - TO BE HEARD AT 9:00 AM ON THE DISCOVERY CALENDAR. ALTHOUGH MS. SAKULTANAPANICH DID NOT STIPULATE TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT THE HEARING ON JULY 26, 2012, THE JUDGE PRO TEM HAD ISSUED HIS TENTATIVE RULING BASED SOLELY ON THE PROCEDURAL GROUND THAT MS. SAKULTANAPANICH HAD NOT PROVIDED COURTESY COPIES OF HER MOTION PER LOCAL RULE 2.6B. AT THE HEARING, MS. SAKULTANAPANICH PRESENTED COURTESY COPIES OF HER MOTION AND REPLY. THE MATTER SHALL NOW BE HEARD BY A JUDGE PRO TEM ON THE MERITS. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976 =(302/CK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM KANE EXCEPT AS TO THOSE ISSUES STATED ON THE RECORD: DEFENDANT ESTELLA SAKULTANAPANICH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES FROM TARGET NATIONAL BANK, FOR SANCTIONS, AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR PRECLUSION IS GRANTED, IN PART, AND DENIED AS TO SANCTIONS. BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES, FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES 1 AND 2 ARE DENIED. WITH RESPECT TO INTERROGATORY 3, BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION, IF PLAINTIFF HAS PRODUCED ALL SUCH DOCUMENTS IT NEEDS TO STATE THAT IN A FURTHER RESPONSE. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO INTERROGATORY 4 & INTERROGATORY #10 ARE SUSTAINED. THE PARTIES SHOULD BE AWARE THAT FAILURE TO DISCLOSE THE NAMES OF WITNESSES OR DOCUMENTS MAY PRECLUDE THEIR INTRODUCTION AT TRIAL. (DO IT URSELF MOVING AND STORAGE, INC. V. BROWN, LEIFER, SLATKIN & BERNS (1992) 7 CAL.APP.4TH 27, 36.) THE COURT ADDS A DUE DATE OF AUGUST 31, 2012 TO SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976 =302/EG

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ESTELLA SAKULTANAPANICH'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES FROM TARGET NATIONAL BANK, FOR SANCTIONS, AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR PRECLUSION - TO BE HEARD AT 9:00 AM ON THE DISCOVERY CALENDAR. ALTHOUGH MS. SAKULTANAPANICH DID NOT STIPULATE TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT THE HEARING ON JULY 26, 2012, THE JUDGE PRO TEM HAD ISSUED HIS TENTATIVE RULING BASED SOLELY ON THE PROCEDURAL GROUND THAT MS. SAKULTANAPANICH HAD NOT PROVIDED COURTESY COPIES OF HER MOTION PER LOCAL RULE 2.6B. AT THE HEARING, MS. SAKULTANAPANICH PRESENTED COURTESY COPIES OF HER MOTION AND REPLY. THE MATTER SHALL NOW BE HEARD BY A JUDGE PRO TEM ON THE MERITS. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM KANE EXCEPT THOSE ISSUES STATED ON THE RECORD: DEFENDANT ESTELLA SAKULTANAPANICH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER REESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM TARGET NATIONAL BANK, FOR SANCTIONS, AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR PRECLUSION IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION ("RFP") NOS. 6, 7, 10 AND DENIED AS TO RFP NOS. 1, 2, 5 AND SANCTIONS. WITH RESPECT TO THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES AND STATEMENT THAT ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN PRODUCED ADDRESS REQUESTS NUMBERS 1, 2, 5 AND THEREFORE THE REQUEST FOR FURTHER RESPONSES IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST #6 FOR PRODUCTION OF CREDIT REPORTS MAY LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE AND AS PLAINTIFF'S HAS NOT ADDRESSED THIS SPECIFIC REQUEST, THE PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST #7 IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY SUCH DOCUMENTS EXIST AS THIS IS RELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER. IN THIS REGARD, DEFENDANT CLAIMS THAT WHILE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT HAVE A APPLICATION (REQUEST #1), IT HAS SOME DOCUMENTATION RELATING TO THE APPLICATION AS IT WAS ABLE TO STATE THE DATE THE ACCOUNT WAS OPEN. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST #10 IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY SUCH DOCUMENTS EXIST AS THIS IS RELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER. THE COURT ADDS A DUE DATE OF AUGUST 31, 2012 TO SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES AND PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM KANE EXCEPT AS TO THOSE ISSUES STATED ON THE RECORD: DEFENDANT ESTELLA SAKULTANAPANICH'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES FROM TARGET NATIONAL BANK, FOR SANCTIONS, AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR PRECLUSION IS GRANTED, IN PART, AND DENIED AS TO SANCTIONS. AS TO INTERROGATORY 150.1, THE DOCUMENT ITSELF SHOULD BE IDENTIFIED AND NOT THE ATTACHMENT OF THE DOCUMENT. ALSO THE CUSTODIAL INFORMATION NEEDS TO BE PROVIDED. SIMILARLY WITH RESPECT TO INTERROGATORY 150.4, PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO STATE IF THERE WERE MODIFICATIONS AND IF SO DESCRIBE OR ATTACH THE SPECIFIC DOCUMENTS AND PROVIDE THE CUSTODIAL INFORMATION. THE PARTIES SHOULD BE AWARE THAT FAILURE TO DISCLOSE THE NAMES OF WITNESSES OR DOCUMENTS MAY PRECLUDE THEIR INTRODUCTION AT TRIAL. (DO IT URSELF MOVING AND STORAGE, INC. V. BROWN, LEIFER, SLATKIN & BERNS (1992) 7 CAL.APP.4TH 27, 36.) THE COURT ADDS A DUE DATE OF AUGUST 31, 2012 TO SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion For Order To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion
Off Calendar Aug-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER LIEDERMAN, TEL. NO. (510) 681-6916, SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ESTELLA SAKULTANAPANICH, TEL. NO. 661-6581, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Kane except as to those
issues stated on the record: Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion For Order To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion is Granted, in part, and denied as to sanctions. As to interrogatory 150.1, the document itself should be identified and not the attachment of the document. Also the custodial information needs to be provided. Similarly with respect to interrogatory 150.4, Plaintiff needs to state if there were modifications and if so describe or attach the specific documents and provide the custodial information. The parties should be aware that failure to disclose the names of witnesses or documents may preclude their introduction at trial. (Do It Urself Moving and Storage, Inc. v. Brown, Leifer, Slatkin & Berns (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 27, 36.) The court adds a due date of August 31, 2012 to serve supplemental responses. Parties to meet and confer as to form of order. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =302/EG

Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion
Off Calendar Aug-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER LIEDERMAN, TEL. NO. (510) 681-6916, SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ESTELLA SAKULTANAPANICH, TEL. NO. 661-6581, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Kane except as to those
issues stated on the record: Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion is Granted, in part, and denied as to sanctions. Based on Plaintiff's supplemental responses, further responses to interrogatories 1 and 2 are denied. With respect to interrogatory 3, based on Plaintiff's opposition, if Plaintiff has produced all such documents it needs to state that in a further response. Plaintiff's objection to interrogatory 4 & interrogatory #10 are sustained. The parties should be aware that failure to disclose the names of witnesses or documents may preclude their introduction at trial. (Do It Urself Moving and Storage, Inc. v. Brown, Leifer, Slatkin & Berns (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 27, 36.) The court adds a due date of August 31, 2012 to serve supplemental responses. Parties to meet and confer as form of order. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =302/EG
=(302/EHG)

Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion To Compel Further Reesponses To Request For Production Of Documents From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion
Off Calendar Aug-09-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER LIEDERMAN, TEL. NO. (510) 681-6916, SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ESTELLA SAKULTANAPANICH, TEL. NO. 661-6581, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM KANE EXCEPT THOSE ISSUES STATED ON THE RECORD: Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion To Compel Further Reesponses To Request For Production Of Documents From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion is Granted as to Request for Production ("RFP") nos. 6, 7, 10 and denied as to RFP nos. 1, 2, 5 and sanctions. With respect to the requests for production, Plaintiff's supplemental responses and statement that all responsive documents have been produced address requests numbers 1, 2, 5 and therefore the request for further responses is denied. Defendant's request #6 for production of credit reports may lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and as Plaintiff's has not addressed this specific request, the Plaintiff is ordered to produce any responsive documents. Defendant's request #7 is granted to the extent that any such documents exist as this is relevant to the subject matter. In this regard, Defendant claims that while Plaintiff does not have a application (request #1), it has some documentation relating to the application as it was able to state the date the account was open. Defendant's request #10 is granted to the extent that any such documents exist as this is relevant to the subject matter. The court adds a due date of August 31, 2012 to serve supplemental responses and produce responsive documents. PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO FORM OF ORDER. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
=(302/EHG)

Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion For Order To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion
Off Calendar Aug-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Estella Sakultanapanich'S Motion For Order To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories From Target National Bank, For Sanctions, And In The Alternative, For Preclusion - To be heard at 9:00 am on the Discovery calendar. Although Ms. Sakultanapanich did not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem at the hearing on July 26, 2012, the Judge Pro Tem had issued his tentative ruling based solely on the procedural ground that Ms. Sakultanapanich had not provided courtesy copies of her motion per Local Rule 2.6B. At the hearing, Ms. Sakultanapanich presented courtesy copies of her motion and reply. The matter shall now be heard by a Judge Pro Tem on the merits. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/CK)
